title: excoecaria pallens var. tenax
intro: Excoecaria pallens var. tenax, also known as the Tenax Ironwood, is a variant of the Excoecaria pallens plant, a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits associated with Excoecaria pallens var. tenax include the potential treatment of fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a natural remedy for snake bites and other poisonous stings. Traditionally, the plant is prepared through herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are believed to enhance its medicinal properties and health benefits.
